Chào bạn, tôi đã hiểu yêu cầu của bạn. Vấn đề cốt lõi ở đây là việc render hàng nghìn phần tử DOM cùng một lúc bằng phương thức `.map()`, gây ra tình trạng "treo" giao diện người dùng. Giải pháp tối ưu nhất chính là áp dụng kỹ thuật "list virtualization".

Tôi sẽ sử dụng thư viện `react-window` kết hợp với `react-virtualized-auto-sizer` để chỉ render những dòng (tags) đang hiển thị trong viewport, giúp ứng dụng chạy mượt mà dù có hàng chục nghìn tags.

1.  **`index.html`**: Thêm thư viện `react-window` và `react-virtualized-auto-sizer` vào `importmap` để có thể sử dụng trong các component.
2.  **`App.tsx`**:
    *   Xóa bỏ dòng `.slice(0, 500)` để hiển thị tất cả các tags đã được quét.
    *   Tăng nhẹ khoảng thời gian throttle trong `renderLoop` từ `150ms` lên `250ms`. Điều này giúp giảm tần suất cập nhật state và re-render khi có một lượng lớn dữ liệu, tối ưu hóa hiệu năng khi sắp xếp một mảng lớn.
3.  **`InteractiveLog.tsx`**:
    *   Thay thế vòng lặp `.map()` bằng component `<FixedSizeList>` từ `react-window`.
    *   Sử dụng `<AutoSizer>` để component list tự động lấp đầy không gian của container cha.
    *   Tạo một component `Row` nội bộ để render từng dòng, giữ nguyên hoàn toàn layout và styling cũ của bạn.

Kết quả là một danh sách có thể cuộn mượt mà, hiệu năng cao và đáp ứng được yêu cầu của bạn.

Service UUID: 0x00FF

Command/Data Characteristic: 0xFF01 (Read, Write, Notify).

Note: All commands are sent to this characteristic. All live data streams are received from this characteristic via Notifications.

2. Command Protocol (JSON UTF-8): Please update/verify the buttons to send these exact JSON commands:

Interactive Mode (Live Stream):

Start: {"cmd":"S"}

Stop: {"cmd":"X"}

Batch Mode (Offline Save):

Start: {"cmd":"SB"}

Stop & Save: {"cmd":"XB"}

Find My Item Mode (NEW V8.0):

Start Finding: {"cmd":"F", "val":"TARGET_EPC_HEX"}

Example: {"cmd":"F", "val":"E280116060000213966A4875"}

Stop Finding: {"cmd":"X"} (Same as Stop Interactive).

3. Data Stream Format (Received via Notify on 0xFF01): The device sends data in batches (max 3 tags per packet).

Format:

JSON
{
  "cmd": "live_tags",
  "tags": [
    { "e": "E28011...", "r": -60 },
    { "e": "E28012...", "r": -75 }
  ]
}
e: EPC Hex String.

r: RSSI (Signal Strength in dBm).

Section 3: Batch Mode (Existing)

Buttons: "Start Batch" / "Stop & Save".

5. Logic Constraints:

Role: Senior React Developer & UI/UX Designer. Task: Build a high-performance React Web Bluetooth application to control an ESP32-S3 UHF RFID Reader. Tech Stack: React (Vite), Tailwind CSS, Lucide-React (Icons), Web Bluetooth API.

Color Palette:

Background: Light Grey (#f2f4f6 / bg-slate-50).

Cards/Panels: Pure White (#ffffff), Rounded corners (rounded-2xl), Soft Shadows (shadow-sm or shadow-md).

Text: Dark Grey (#171a20) for headings, Muted Grey (#5c5e62) for labels.

Primary Action: Tesla Blue (#3e6ae1).

Success: Green (#10a37f).

Danger: Red (#e82127).

Typography: Inter or system-sans, clean and legible.

2. BLE Protocol Specifications (Firmware V8.0)
Service UUID: 0x00FF

Characteristic UUID: 0xFF01 (Read, Write, Notify).

Data Flow:

Commands (App -> Device): Send JSON strings (UTF-8).

Stream (Device -> App): Receive JSON strings via Notifications.

Command Reference:

Live Scan: Start: {"cmd":"S"}, Stop: {"cmd":"X"}

Find Mode: Start: {"cmd":"F", "val":"TARGET_EPC_HEX"}, Stop: {"cmd":"X"}

Batch Mode: Start: {"cmd":"SB"}, Stop/Save: {"cmd":"XB"}

Write Tag: {"cmd":"WE", "old":"OLD_EPC", "new":"NEW_EPC"}

Config:

Power: {"cmd":"SP", "val":INT} (0-33)

Region: {"cmd":"SF", "val":"STRING"} (US/EU)

Buzzer: {"cmd":"BZ", "val":"on"} / off

Baseband: {"cmd":"SRP", "val":"speed,q,sess,targ"}

Get Info: {"cmd":"DI"}, {"cmd":"GP"}, etc.

Incoming Data Format:

JSON
{
  "cmd": "live_tags",
  "tags": [
    { "e": "E28011...", "r": -60 },
    { "e": "E28012...", "r": -75 }
  ]
}
3. Functional Requirements (Tabs)
The app should have a Sidebar navigation and the following functional views:

A. Live Monitor (Dashboard)
Controls: "Start Stream" / "Stop".

Metrics: Cards showing Total Tags, Read Rate (tags/sec), Peak RSSI.

Data Table:

Columns: #, EPC (Click to copy), Signal Bar (Visual progress bar based on RSSI), RSSI Value, Last Seen.

Logic: Deduplicate tags based on EPC. Update RSSI and timestamp if tag exists. Sort by 'Last Seen'.

B. Find Item (Geiger Counter)
Input: Target EPC field (Paste or type).

Workflow:

Step 1: Scan Nearby: Button triggers a short Live Scan (3s) to populate a list of nearby tags.

Step 2: Select: User clicks a tag from the list -> Populates "Current EPC" input.

Step 3: Write: User enters "New EPC" -> Clicks "Write Tag".

Validation: Ensure New EPC is hex and correct length.

D. Settings (Hardware Config)
Controls:

RF Power Slider (0-33 dBm).

Region Dropdown (FCC/ETSI).

Buzzer Toggle.

Advanced Baseband: Q-Value and Session dropdowns.

Logic: Auto-fetch current settings on connect.

4. React Implementation Details
Use react-use-bluetooth hook pattern or standard Web Bluetooth API.

Use shadcn/ui concepts (clean components) using Tailwind classes directly.

State Management: Maintain a device state, connectionStatus, and a tags map.

Error Handling: Show Toasts/Alerts for connection failures or write errors.

Advanced React Web Bluetooth App for RFID Handheld (ScannerOS v11)
Role: Expert React Developer & UI/UX Designer specialized in Industrial IoT Dashboard. Goal: Build a complete React (Vite + Tailwind CSS) Web Bluetooth application to control an ESP32-S3 UHF RFID Handheld running Firmware V8.0.

1. Design Language: "Tesla White" (Model 3/Y UI)
Theme: Modern, Minimalist, Automotive-grade clarity.

Colors:

Background: #f2f4f6 (Light Grey).

Cards: #ffffff (Pure White) with soft shadows (shadow-sm).

Text: #171a20 (Dark Grey), #5c5e62 (Medium Grey).

Accents: #3e6ae1 (Tesla Blue) for Active states/Buttons.

Layout: Sidebar Navigation (Left) + Main Content Area (Right).

2. Data Protocol & Commands
Service UUID: 0x00FF

Characteristic UUID: 0xFF01 (Read/Write/Notify).

Command Mapping (App -> Device):

Start/Stop: {"cmd":"S"} / {"cmd":"X"} / {"cmd":"F", "val":"EPC"}.

RF Power: {"cmd":"SP", "val":INT} (Range: 0-33 dBm).

Region/Freq: {"cmd":"SF", "val":"STRING"} (Values: "US", "EU", "CN").

Baseband: {"cmd":"SRP", "val":"speed,q,session,target"}.

Note: val is a comma-separated string (e.g., "1,4,0,0").

Buzzer: {"cmd":"BZ", "val":"on"} or off.

System Info: {"cmd":"DI"} (Device Info), {"cmd":"GP"} (Get Power), etc.

Incoming Data (Device -> App): The app must handle JSON streams. Example:

JSON
// Tag Stream
{"cmd":"live_tags", "tags":[{"e":"E280...","r":-60}]}

// System Status (Future Proofing)
// Even if firmware sends dummy values now, prepare UI to parse:
{"cmd":"status", "bat": 85, "temp": 42}
3. UI Requirements & Tabs
Global Header (Always Visible)
Left: Brand Logo ("ScannerOS").

Right:

Hardware Stats: Display Battery % (e.g., "85%") and Chip Temp (e.g., "42°C"). Note: Initialize these with dummy values if no data is received yet.

Connection Badge: Disconnected (Grey) / Connected (Green).

Connect Button.

Tab 1: Live Monitor
HUD Cards: Total Tags, Tags/Sec, Peak RSSI.

Table: Clean table with sticky header. Columns: #, EPC (Monospace), Signal Bar (Visual), RSSI, Time.

Tab 2: Find Item
Input: Large input for Target EPC.

Visual: A horizontal "Energy Bar" (Tesla style) filling from 0-100% based on RSSI (-90 to -30 dBm).

Logic: Send {"cmd":"F"} on start. Auto-clear bar if no signal for 2s.

Tab 3: Tag Encoder
Scan & Select: Scan nearby tags -> Click to select "Old EPC".

Write: Input "New EPC" -> Button sends {"cmd":"WE", "old":"...", "new":"..."}.

Tab 4: Settings (CRITICAL UPDATE)
This tab must be detailed controls for RF parameters.

Card 1: Power & Frequency

Output Power: Range Slider (0-33 dBm) with direct value input.

Region: Select (FCC US, ETSI EU, China).

Buzzer: Toggle Switch.

Card 2: RF Baseband (Advanced)

Q-Value: Select (0 to 15). Tooltip: "Collision arbitration".

Session: Select (S0, S1, S2, S3). Tooltip: "Inventory persistence".

Target: Select (A, B, A/B). Tooltip: "Tag flag state".

Speed (Tari): Select (0=Auto, 1=Fast, etc - map to 0-4).

Action Button: "Apply Baseband Config" -> Sends SRP command.

4. Technical Implementation
Framework: React (Vite).

Styling: Tailwind CSS (No external CSS files).

Icons: lucide-react.

State: Use useState for UI and useRef for BLE cache to ensure performance (60fps).

Robustness: Handle JSON parsing errors gracefully (try-catch).

Output: Generate the full App.jsx code containing all components, logic, and styles.

The code changes have been applied. I addressed the "read count" bug by implementing immutable state updates and stabilized the list order in Batch mode by sorting based on discovery time (`firstSeen`) rather than update time.

Here are the specific updates:

1.  **`types.ts`**: Added `firstSeen` to the `Tag` interface.
2.  **`App.tsx`**:
    *   Refactored `handleDataReceived` to create new object copies when updating tags (fixing the count reactivity issue).
    *   Updated `sortedBatchTags` to sort by `firstSeen` descending, ensuring the list order remains stable during scanning.

entries.description: Added 'firstSeen' to Tag interface to allow for stable sorting (by discovery time) instead of jumpy last-seen sorting in list views.

Fixed the issue where tag counts were not updating by implementing immutable state updates for tags. Changed Batch view to sort by 'firstSeen' to prevent the list from jumping continuously, ensuring a stable and readable interactive list.
